title: hypericum goyanesii
intro: Hypericum goyanesii, also known as Tawa, is a perennial shrub native to the Andean region in South America, belonging to the St. John's Wort family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Hypericum goyanesii include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as exhibiting antiviral and antifungal properties, which can aid in the treatment of various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are typically used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
